title: Accounting Rate of Return
event: Reyes Co.'s proposed $240,000 machine (no salvage) promises annual net income of $21,000. Compute the accounting rate of return.

computationPaths.narration: ARR is the odd one in the chapter: it runs on NET INCOME and AVERAGE book investment, not cash.

memorize.body: ARR = annual net INCOME ÷ average investment. Average investment = (cost + salvage) ÷ 2.
Two traps in one ratio: income (not cash flow) on top, AVERAGE (not full cost) on the bottom.

questions.prompt: Annual net income $21,000, cost $240,000, no salvage. ARR = ? (%)

distractors.feedback: You divided by the FULL 240,000. ARR uses AVERAGE investment: (240,000 + 0) ÷ 2 = 120,000 → 17.5%.
You used cash flow instead of net income on top. ARR is the accounting measure: income ÷ average investment.
